title: Meridian US Equity Growth Fund
content:
MERIDIAN US EQUITY GROWTH FUND - PRODUCT FACTSHEET

Fund Overview
The Meridian US Equity Growth Fund (MUEGF) is an actively managed equity strategy designed to capitalize on long-term capital appreciation opportunities within the US domestic market. The fund primarily invests in growth-oriented companies with market capitalizations exceeding $2 billion, focusing on sectors demonstrating sustainable competitive advantages and above-average earnings growth potential.

Investment Objective
The fund seeks to achieve long-term capital appreciation by investing at least 80% of its net assets in US equity securities. The portfolio targets companies with projected annual earnings growth rates of 15% or higher over a three-year horizon, with particular emphasis on technology, healthcare innovation, and consumer discretionary sectors.

Performance Summary (as of March 15, 2025)
- Year-to-Date Return: 8.74%
- 1-Year Return: 22.31%
- 3-Year Annualized Return: 18.96%
- 5-Year Annualized Return: 16.42%
- Inception-to-Date (since 2018): 19.78% annualized
- Benchmark (Russell 1000 Growth): 17.23% (5-year annualized)

Fund Characteristics
- Total Net Assets: $847.3 million
- Number of Holdings: 42
- Portfolio Turnover: 67% (trailing 12 months)
- Active Share: 84.2%
- Expense Ratio: 0.89% (Class A shares)
- Management Fee: 0.65%
- Minimum Initial Investment: $25,000

Top Holdings (as of last reporting period)
1. Microsoft Corporation (MSFT) - 7.8%
2. NVIDIA Corporation (NVDA) - 6.4%
3. Amazon.com Inc (AMZN) - 5.9%
4. Tesla Inc (TSLA) - 4.7%
5. Apple Inc (AAPL) - 4.3%

Sector Allocation
- Information Technology: 38.5%
- Healthcare: 18.2%
- Consumer Discretionary: 16.7%
- Communication Services: 12.1%
- Industrials: 8.9%
- Other: 5.6%

Risk Profile and Suitability
This fund carries an aggressive risk classification due to its concentrated portfolio construction and growth-oriented mandate. Standard deviation over the past three years measures 21.4%, with a beta of 1.23 relative to the S&P 500. The strategy exhibits higher volatility than broad market indices and may experience significant drawdowns during market corrections.

Regulatory and Compliance Considerations
Consistent with FINRA Rule 2111 suitability requirements, this product is appropriate for investors with aggressive risk tolerance, extended investment horizons (5+ years), and capacity to withstand principal fluctuations of 25% or greater. All client communications and transaction records are maintained in accordance with SEC Rule 17a-4 record retention standards, with electronic storage systems meeting regulatory specifications for non-rewriteable, non-erasable formats.

Fee Structure
- Class A Shares: 5.75% front-end load, 0.89% annual expense ratio
- Class B Shares: No front-end load, 1.64% annual expense ratio, 5% CDSC (declining)
- Class I Shares (Institutional): No load, 0.64% annual expense ratio, $1MM minimum

Tax Considerations
The fund's active management approach and 67% turnover rate may generate taxable distributions. Historical capital gains distributions have averaged 4.2% of net asset value annually. Tax-sensitive investors should consider holding this fund in qualified retirement accounts.

Distribution and Availability
Available through Meridian Wealth Partners registered representatives and qualified third-party platforms. All sales require completed risk assessment documentation and signed customer account agreements. Fund shares are offered by prospectus only.
